name: toolkit-leopoldo
description: Curated engineering toolkit: opinionated stack of plugins for the full development pipeline (alignment → PRD → vertical-slice issues → AFK handoff → TDD execution → architectural sanity → triage), plus standalone alignment and response-mode tools. Adapted in part from mattpocock/skills (MIT).

plugins.name: engineering-workflow
plugins.description: Unified engineering workflow (12 skills + /init-workflow command). The full pipeline: /grill-with-docs and /context-bootstrap (alignment + shared language), /to-prd, /to-issues, /agent-brief, /triage (backlog flow), /tdd-vertical, /diagnose, /zoom-out, /deep-modules (disciplined coding), /prototype (throwaway design exploration), /review-fleet (multi-agent review: parallel reviewers per module → judge rules APLICAR/RECHAZAR/HUMANO; report-only by default, --apply runs appliers on best model). Run /init-workflow once per repo to seed the pipeline order in CLAUDE.md. v2.4.0: adopts upstream `prototype` skill (mattpocock/skills) verbatim — routes between a terminal TUI for state/logic prototypes and N radically different UI variants on one route for visual prototypes. v2.3.0: /to-issues generates the agent brief inline for every AFK slice; /agent-brief gains Reality-anchored principle + Real resources section validating refs against .sandcastle/probes/*.schema.

plugins.name: handoff
plugins.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up. Saves a summary to the OS temp directory with a 'suggested skills' section, references existing artifacts (PRDs, plans, ADRs, issues, diffs) instead of duplicating them, and redacts secrets. Standalone — sibling of grill-me. Adopted verbatim from mattpocock/skills (MIT).

plugins.name: caveman
plugins.description: Ultra-compressed response mode as a SKILL (not output-style). Cuts ~75% tokens by dropping articles/filler/pleasantries while keeping full technical accuracy. Persists after trigger until user revokes. Auto-clarity exception for security warnings and destructive ops. Adopted verbatim from mattpocock/skills (MIT). Coexists with response-modes' caveman output-style — pick whichever activation model fits.

plugins.name: grill-me
plugins.description: Stress-test plans through relentless interviewing — walks the decision tree branch by branch, one question at a time, exploring the codebase when answers live there. Triggers: 'grill me', 'pressure test', 'poke holes', 'play devil's advocate', 'validar plan', 'discutir antes de implementar'. Standalone — useful for non-code planning sessions where engineering-workflow is overkill.

plugins.name: interface-lens
plugins.description: Juzgar, diseñar y construir interfaces con psicología UX + brújula ética (eje ético → persuasivo → oscuro, prueba del arrepentimiento de Nir Eyal, norte time-well-spent). Base de conocimiento: 28 principios en 6 familias (disparadores, acción/fricción, recompensa, inversión, claridad, ética) organizados como bucle disparador→acción→recompensa→inversión, más taxonomía operativa de dark patterns con señales de código por patrón. Tres skills de invocación explícita: /ui-judge audita screenshot/URL/código con hallazgos anclados a evidencia concreta (elemento/cita/archivo:línea — nunca heurísticas genéricas), score por familia con veto ético, y sección obligatoria de límites del input; /ux-design propone flujos declarando qué principio usa cada decisión y por qué sirve al usuario, con guardas éticas y salida simétrica a la entrada; /dark-pattern-scan escanea flujos completos pre-ship (suscripción, checkout, consentimiento) midiendo asimetría de fricción entrada/salida, con reporte pasa/falla, banderas regulatorias (FTC click-to-cancel, DSA) e integración opcional con /code-review. Complementa (no solapa) skills de diseño visual tipo ui-ux-pro-max: esto cubre cómo se comporta la interfaz y su ética, no cómo se ve.

plugins.name: memory-flow
plugins.description: Barrido batch de memoria: /revisar_memoria clasifica memorias del formato por-archivo (VIGENTE/STALE/GRADUABLE/OBSOLETA/FUSIONABLE) y propone graduaciones a CLAUDE.md/CONTEXT.md. Solo reporta; aplica con OK explícito. Complemento del flujo orgánico (graduar/corregir al tocar) — v2 elimina /actualizar_memoria y el agente auditor.

plugins.name: review-flow
plugins.description: Checkpoints pre y post ejecución: /analizar verifica comprensión antes de ejecutar (ideal para dictados por voz o instrucciones ambiguas); /revisar_trabajo aplica revisión crítica post-implementación. Incluye agent revisor_de_trabajo para análisis exhaustivos sin modificar código.

plugins.name: response-modes
plugins.description: Optional output styles for response tone/format. Caveman: ultra-terse, drops filler. No-TLDR: overrides the always-on TL;DR rule for fluid chat. Toggle via /output-style or settings.

plugins.name: host-orchestrator
plugins.description: Host-side orchestration of the full AFK pipeline. v4.0.0 (BREAKING): the pipeline engine is now a deterministic Workflow script (`workflows/prd-pipeline.js`, launched by the new `/prd-pipeline` command) — rules run as JS code, agents only implement/measure/resolve. Gate = pure `if` over validator numbers (generic ratchet: no metric worsens vs wave baseline, no before-green test goes red, diff must touch tests; invalid measurement is NEVER success). Integration-branch policy: creates `prd/<milestone>` (or `batch/<slug>`), issue PRs target it, base merged in before each wave, run ends with ONE draft PR to base for Leo's green button. All remote mutations serialized through idempotent check-then-act serializer agents (work-identity keyed, audit-logged). Capability tiers T0-T3 instead of model names (mapping only in repo config `model_map`); the launching session is the T0 orchestrator and pins each node's tier at design time. Native review fleet phase (partition → reviewers → judge → applier → gate). Resume via Workflow journal: `resumeFromRunId` only if nothing changed by hand, fresh run always safe. Spec: docs/SPEC-v4-workflow-engine.md (grilled 2026-07-16, validated by Piloto 1 ratchet-TS 2026-07-17: 6/6 criteria, 3 resumes without duplicated mutations). REMOVED in v4.0.0: `/afk-pipeline`, `/goal` wrapping, state.json, PROGRESS.md, cc-afk env-var scaffolding (only Bash/API timeouts survive). Kept as standalone surgical tools: `/parallel-implement-wave` (2-6 issues, parallel worktree subagents) and `/merge-orchestrate` (2-7 PRs, serial intent-aware merge). Custom subagents: `parallel-implementer` (TDD vertical-slice discipline, never pushes) and `merge-resolver` (5 no-regression criteria, recommends — serializer executes). Explicit invocation only; the Workflow tool's own opt-in gate doubles the marketplace rule.

plugins.name: sandcastle-max
plugins.description: AFK Claude Code agents en Docker contra tu Claude Max subscription (vía CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, workaround para Sandcastle issue #191 wontfix). v0.9.1: bugfix — Claude Code guarda credenciales en Keychain como JSON wrapper `{"claudeAiOauth":{"accessToken":...}}`; los comandos extraían el JSON crudo y el container devolvía `401 Invalid bearer token`. Ahora todos los comandos (sandcastle-run, sandcastle-dispatch-wave, sandcastle-pipeline, sandcastle-merge-wave) y la skill `sandcastle-afk` parsean con jq y exportan solo `.claudeAiOauth.accessToken`, con fallback a token raw para compat. v0.9.0: API declarativa para Docker capabilities/devices/post-create hook vía bloque `docker` en .sandcastle/config.json — habilita Netbird-in-container (NET_ADMIN + /dev/net/tun + post-create hook) para que AFK agents alcancen recursos VPN-only (ej: stand-by DB) durante su loop, evitando queries blind contra schemas imaginados. Patch al @ai-hero/sandcastle@0.5.10 incluido en runtime/patches/, aplicado via bun.patchedDependencies en el primer /sandcastle-init. v0.8.0 sigue vigente: bootstrap del schema cache standalone vía /sandcastle-probe-resources (host-only, no containers) — cachea schemas a .sandcastle/probes/<name>.schema que engineering-workflow >=2.3.0 lee desde /to-issues y /agent-brief para anchor briefs a refs reales (verifica table.column, endpoint, topic, key). v0.7.0: .sandcastle/resources.json declara recursos externos (DB/HTTP/queue/etc) con policy mandatory|optional; 3 niveles de defensa (Level 1 host pre-flight, Level 2 container connectivity, Level 3 container schema diff); runtime/main.mts propaga env_required al agentEnv; prompt enforza red-green-reality-first. v0.6.0 stack-aware scaffold: Dockerfile compuesto per-project desde snippets (Node/Bun/.NET/Python/Go/Ruby/Rust/polyglot) + agent layer con UID surgery. Slash commands: /sandcastle-init, /sandcastle-probe-resources, /sandcastle-build, /sandcastle-run, /sandcastle-dispatch-wave, /sandcastle-merge-wave, /sandcastle-pipeline. Per-project image: sandcastle-<repo-basename>.
